authors_text: Yao Yao,Junyi Shen,Jin Xu,Bin Zhong,Li Xiao
title: CLS: Cross Labeling Supervision for Semi-Supervised Learning
title_zh: CLS：半监督学习的交叉标记监督
abstract: It is well known that the success of deep neural networks is greatly attributed to large-scale labeled datasets. However, it can be extremely time-consuming and laborious to collect sufficient high-quality labeled data in most practical applications. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) provides an effective solution to reduce the cost of labeling by simultaneously leveraging both labeled and unlabeled data. In this work, we present Cross Labeling Supervision (CLS), a framework that generalizes the typical pseudo-labeling process. Based on FixMatch, where a pseudo label is generated from a weakly-augmented sample to teach the prediction on a strong augmentation of the same input sample, CLS allows the creation of both pseudo and complementary labels to support both positive and negative learning. To mitigate the confirmation bias of self-labeling and boost the tolerance to false labels, two different initialized networks with the same structure are trained simultaneously. Each network utilizes high-confidence labels from the other network as additional supervision signals. During the label generation phase, adaptive sample weights are assigned to artificial labels according to their prediction confidence. The sample weight plays two roles: quantify the generated labels' quality and reduce the disruption of inaccurate labels on network training. Experimental results on the semi-supervised classification task show that our framework outperforms existing approaches by large margins on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets.
summary: It is well known that the success of deep neural networks is greatly attributed to large-scale labeled datasets. However, it can be extremely time-consuming and laborious to collect sufficient high-quality labeled data in most practical applications. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) provides an effective solution to reduce the cost of labeling by simultaneously leveraging both labeled and unlabeled data. In this work, we present Cross Labeling Supervision (CLS), a framework that generalizes the typical pseudo-labeling process. Based on FixMatch, where a pseudo label is generated from a weakly-augmented sample to teach the prediction on a strong augmentation of the same input sample, CLS allows the creation of both pseudo and complementary labels to support both positive and negative learning. To mitigate the confirmation bias of self-labeling and boost the tolerance to false labels, two different initialized networks with the same structure are trained simultaneously. Each network utilizes high-confidence labels from the other network as additional supervision signals. During the label generation phase, adaptive sample weights are assigned to artificial labels according to their prediction confidence. The sample weight plays two roles: quantify the generated labels' quality and reduce the disruption of inaccurate labels on network training. Experimental results on the semi-supervised classification task show that our framework outperforms existing approaches by large margins on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，深度神经网络的成功很大程度上归功于大规模标记数据集。然而，在大多数实际应用中，收集足够的高质量标记数据可能非常耗时和费力。半监督学习 (SSL) 提供了一种有效的解决方案，通过同时利用标记和未标记数据来降低标记成本。在这项工作中，我们提出了交叉标签监督（CLS），这是一个概括了典型伪标签过程的框架。基于 FixMatch，其中从弱增强样本生成伪标签，以教导对同一输入样本的强增强的预测，CLS 允许创建伪标签和互补标签以支持正学习和负学习。为了减轻自标记的确认偏差并提高对错误标签的容忍度，同时训练两个具有相同结构的不同初始化网络。每个网络都利用来自其他网络的高可信度标签作为额外的监督信号。在标签生成阶段，根据预测置信度将自适应样本权重分配给人工标签。样本权重有两个作用：量化生成标签的质量并减少不准确标签对网络训练的干扰。半监督分类任务的实验结果表明，我们的框架在 CIFAR-10 和 CIFAR-100 数据集上大幅优于现有方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CLS的跨标签监督框架，用于改进半监督学习中的伪标签生成过程。
AI.motivation: 深度学习依赖大规模标注数据，但标注成本高昂。现有半监督方法如FixMatch存在确认偏误和对错误标签容忍度低的问题。
AI.method: 通过同时训练两个结构相同但初始化不同的网络，互相提供高置信度标签作为监督信号，并根据预测置信度分配自适应样本权重以提升标签质量。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上，CLS框架显著优于现有半监督分类方法。
AI.conclusion: CLS通过跨网络标签校验和动态权重机制有效缓解了自标注偏差，提升了半监督学习性能。

motivation: 深度学习依赖大规模标注数据，但标注成本高昂。现有半监督方法如FixMatch存在确认偏误和对错误标签容忍度低的问题。
method: 通过同时训练两个结构相同但初始化不同的网络，互相提供高置信度标签作为监督信号，并根据预测置信度分配自适应样本权重以提升标签质量。
result: 在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上，CLS框架显著优于现有半监督分类方法。
conclusion: CLS通过跨网络标签校验和动态权重机制有效缓解了自标注偏差，提升了半监督学习性能。
search_text: CLS: Cross Labeling Supervision for Semi-Supervised Learning CLS：半监督学习的交叉标记监督 It is well known that the success of deep neural networks is greatly attributed to large-scale labeled datasets. However, it can be extremely time-consuming and laborious to collect sufficient high-quality labeled data in most practical applications. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) provides an effective solution to reduce the cost of labeling by simultaneously leveraging both labeled and unlabeled data. In this work, we present Cross Labeling Supervision (CLS), a framework that generalizes the typical pseudo-labeling process. Based on FixMatch, where a pseudo label is generated from a weakly-augmented sample to teach the prediction on a strong augmentation of the same input sample, CLS allows the creation of both pseudo and complementary labels to support both positive and negative learning. To mitigate the confirmation bias of self-labeling and boost the tolerance to false labels, two different initialized networks with the same structure are trained simultaneously. Each network utilizes high-confidence labels from the other network as additional supervision signals. During the label generation phase, adaptive sample weights are assigned to artificial labels according to their prediction confidence. The sample weight plays two roles: quantify the generated labels' quality and reduce the disruption of inaccurate labels on network training. Experimental results on the semi-supervised classification task show that our framework outperforms existing approaches by large margins on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets. 众所周知，深度神经网络的成功很大程度上归功于大规模标记数据集。然而，在大多数实际应用中，收集足够的高质量标记数据可能非常耗时和费力。半监督学习 (SSL) 提供了一种有效的解决方案，通过同时利用标记和未标记数据来降低标记成本。在这项工作中，我们提出了交叉标签监督（CLS），这是一个概括了典型伪标签过程的框架。基于 FixMatch，其中从弱增强样本生成伪标签，以教导对同一输入样本的强增强的预测，CLS 允许创建伪标签和互补标签以支持正学习和负学习。为了减轻自标记的确认偏差并提高对错误标签的容忍度，同时训练两个具有相同结构的不同初始化网络。每个网络都利用来自其他网络的高可信度标签作为额外的监督信号。在标签生成阶段，根据预测置信度将自适应样本权重分配给人工标签。样本权重有两个作用：量化生成标签的质量并减少不准确标签对网络训练的干扰。半监督分类任务的实验结果表明，我们的框架在 CIFAR-10 和 CIFAR-100 数据集上大幅优于现有方法。 本文提出了一种名为CLS的跨标签监督框架，用于改进半监督学习中的伪标签生成过程。 Yao Yao,Junyi Shen,Jin Xu,Bin Zhong,Li Xiao cs.CV

authors_text: Fengze Li,Jieming Ma,Zhongbei Tian,Ji Ge,Hai-Ning Liang,Yungang Zhang,Tianxi Wen
title: Mirror-Yolo: A Novel Attention Focus, Instance Segmentation and Mirror Detection Model
title_zh: Mirror-Yolo：一种新颖的注意力焦点、实例分割和镜像检测模型
abstract: Mirrors can degrade the performance of computer vision models, but research into detecting them is in the preliminary phase. YOLOv4 achieves phenomenal results in terms of object detection accuracy and speed, but it still fails in detecting mirrors. Thus, we propose Mirror-YOLO, which targets mirror detection, containing a novel attention focus mechanism for features acquisition, a hypercolumn-stairstep approach to better fusion the feature maps, and the mirror bounding polygons for instance segmentation. Compared to the existing mirror detection networks and YOLO series, our proposed network achieves superior performance in average accuracy on our proposed mirror dataset and another state-of-art mirror dataset, which demonstrates the validity and effectiveness of Mirror-YOLO.
summary: Mirrors can degrade the performance of computer vision models, but research into detecting them is in the preliminary phase. YOLOv4 achieves phenomenal results in terms of object detection accuracy and speed, but it still fails in detecting mirrors. Thus, we propose Mirror-YOLO, which targets mirror detection, containing a novel attention focus mechanism for features acquisition, a hypercolumn-stairstep approach to better fusion the feature maps, and the mirror bounding polygons for instance segmentation. Compared to the existing mirror detection networks and YOLO series, our proposed network achieves superior performance in average accuracy on our proposed mirror dataset and another state-of-art mirror dataset, which demonstrates the validity and effectiveness of Mirror-YOLO.
abstract_zh: 镜子会降低计算机视觉模型的性能，但检测它们的研究还处于初步阶段。 YOLOv4在物体检测精度和速度方面取得了惊人的成绩，但在检测镜子方面仍然失败。因此，我们提出了 Mirror-YOLO，它的目标是镜像检测，包含用于特征获取的新颖的注意力聚焦机制、用于更好地融合特征图的超列阶梯方法以及用于实例分割的镜像边界多边形。与现有的镜像检测网络和 YOLO 系列相比，我们提出的网络在我们提出的镜像数据集和另一个最先进的镜像数据集上实现了平均精度的优越性能，这证明了 Mirror-YOLO 的有效性和有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Mirror-YOLO模型，专门用于解决计算机视觉中镜子检测的挑战，并通过注意力机制和特征融合方法提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有目标检测模型（如YOLOv4）在镜子检测上表现不佳，而镜子的存在会显著影响视觉任务性能，亟需针对性解决方案。
AI.method: 设计新型注意力聚焦机制提取特征，采用超列阶梯法融合多尺度特征图，并引入镜面边界框实现实例分割。
AI.result: 在作者构建的镜面数据集及现有先进数据集上，Mirror-YOLO的平均准确率均优于现有镜面检测网络和YOLO系列模型。
AI.conclusion: Mirror-YOLO通过创新方法有效解决了镜面检测难题，验证了其在准确率和鲁棒性方面的优势。
